title: palmijuncus dioicus
side_effects: Palmijuncus dioicus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interaction with certain medications. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure may lead to headaches and fatigue. Some individuals may experience skin irritation or rashes due to contact with the plant's sap or leaves. Overuse or misuse of Palmijuncus dioicus may also exacerbate underlying health conditions, such as high blood pressure or kidney disease.
